steps.title: Use equal base angles
steps.content: Since $AB=AC$, triangle $ABC$ is isosceles. The angles opposite those equal sides are equal, so angle $ABC$ and angle $ACB$ each measure $67^\circ$.

steps.title: Use the triangle sum
steps.content: The three interior angles of a triangle sum to $180^\circ$. $$x+67^\circ+67^\circ=180^\circ$$ $$x+134^\circ=180^\circ$$ $$x=46$$ The answer is B.
